8296	\end{lemma}

8300	  \(\operatorname{Pi.lift}\) of the \(\operatorname{some}\) projections; the inverse is
8301	  \(\operatorname{Pi.lift}\) of the matching projections out of the binary product. The round-trip
8302	  identities follow from the universal properties of the products.
8303	\end{proof}

8305	\begin{lemma}[Push--pull on the empty coproduct is terminal]
8306	  \label{lem:pushPull_coprod_prod_empty}
8307	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.pushPull_coprod_prod_empty}
8308	  \uses{def:push_pull_obj, def:coprodToProdMap}

8310	  \(\coprod_{i} \mathrm{legs}\,i\) is the initial scheme \(\bot\); the push--pull object on its unique
8311	  structure map is terminal in \(X.\mathrm{Modules}\), hence isomorphic to the empty product
8312	  \(\prod_{i \in \operatorname{PEmpty}} \operatorname{pushPullObj}\mathcal{F}\,(\mathrm{legs}\,i)\),
8313	  which is likewise terminal:
8314	  \[
8315	    \operatorname{pushPullObj}\mathcal{F}\,\Bigl(\operatorname{Over.mk}\bigl(
8316	      \operatorname{Sigma.desc}(i \mapsto (\mathrm{legs}\,i).\mathrm{hom})\bigr)\Bigr)
8317	      \;\cong\;
8318	    \prod_{i \in \operatorname{PEmpty}} \operatorname{pushPullObj}\mathcal{F}\,(\mathrm{legs}\,i).
8319	  \]
8320	\end{lemma}
8321	\begin{proof}
8322	  \uses{def:push_pull_obj, def:coprodToProdMap}
8323	  The coproduct over the empty index type is the initial scheme \(\bot\): the structure map
8324	  \(\operatorname{Sigma.desc}\) out of \(\coprod_{i \in \operatorname{PEmpty}} (\mathrm{legs}\,i).
8325	  \mathrm{left}\) is the unique morphism \(q : \bot \to X\) out of the initial object. Every sheaf of
8326	  modules over the empty scheme is the zero object: the only open of \(\bot\) is the empty open, over
8327	  which the structure sheaf has subsingleton (hence zero) sections, so every
8328	  \(\mathcal{O}_\bot\)-module has zero sections over every open and is therefore the zero module sheaf.
8329	  In particular the pulled-back module \((\operatorname{pullback} q).\mathrm{obj}\,\mathcal{F}\) is the
8330	  zero object of \(\bot.\mathrm{Modules}\).
8331	
8332	  The push--pull object is \(\operatorname{pushPullObj}\mathcal{F}\,(\operatorname{Over.mk} q) =
8333	  (\operatorname{pushforward} q).\mathrm{obj}\bigl((\operatorname{pullback} q).\mathrm{obj}\,
8334	  \mathcal{F}\bigr)\). The module pushforward is additive, so it sends the zero object to a zero
8335	  object, which is terminal. The empty product \(\prod_{i \in \operatorname{PEmpty}}
8336	  \operatorname{pushPullObj}\mathcal{F}\,(\mathrm{legs}\,i)\) is likewise terminal. A morphism between
8337	  terminal objects is an isomorphism, so the comparison map
8338	  \(\operatorname{coprodToProdMap}\mathcal{F}\,\mathrm{legs}\) (Definition~\ref{def:coprodToProdMap}) is
